title: Whirlpool Stock Is Down 20% and the Outlook For 2026 Is Grim. Should Investor Buy, Sell, or Hold?
author: Leo Sun
description: Whirlpool's stock has declined over 40% this year amid a struggling housing market, rising tariffs, and intense competition from Asian appliance makers. The company expects 2026 revenue to decline 3% and adjusted EPS to drop 44%-52%, and has suspended its dividend for the first time in 70 years. Despite a seemingly low valuation at 13x earnings, analysts recommend selling or avoiding the stock until the housing market rebounds and the company resumes dividend payments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock down 40% this year with significant headwinds including declining revenue (from $21.99B to $15.52B since 2021), expected 44-52% EPS decline in 2026, suspended dividend for first time in 70 years, weak housing market demand, elevated inflation, and tariff pressures. Analyst recommendation is to sell or avoid rather than buy.

title: GE Appliances Named 2026 “Smart Appliance Company of the Year”
author: Iot Breakthrough
description: GE Appliances, a Haier company, has been recognized as 'Smart Appliance Company of the Year' at the 10th annual IoT Breakthrough Awards, marking its ninth consecutive win. The award recognizes the company's innovation in smart home technology, particularly its SmartHQ platform that integrates AI-powered assistants, energy efficiency solutions, and connected appliances to enhance daily routines and sustainability.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: As the parent company of GE Appliances, Haier benefits from the subsidiary's continued industry recognition and innovation leadership. The award reflects positively on Haier's portfolio strength and strategic investments in IoT and smart home technology.

title: Japan Water Heater Market Overview and Forecast (2025-2033) Featuring Analysis of A.O. Smith, Rinnai, Whirlpool, Bajaj Electricals, Haier and Other Key Players
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: Japan's water heater market is projected to grow to $2.2 billion by 2033, driven by eco-friendly technologies, government incentives, and rising environmental awareness, despite challenges in urban space constraints and skilled installation.

title: China's Aux Electric Files For Hong Kong IPO
author: Bamboo Works Benzinga Contributor
description: Aux Electric, a Chinese air conditioner manufacturer, has filed for a Hong Kong IPO after multiple previous listing attempts. Known for its low-price strategy, the company has achieved 20% revenue growth in 2024 but faces challenges with lower profit margins compared to industry rivals.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Larger market player with higher profit margins and stable business performance
Large market player with moderate revenue growth and higher profit margins

title: Top Five Trends in Major Appliances: Subscription Models and Direct Sales are Reshaping Customer Relationships, Offering Recurring Revenue and Loyalty Opportunities
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The major appliances market is rebounding, driven by AI, sustainability, and servitization. Beko Corp emerges as the number two global player, challenging market leaders. Subscription models and direct sales are reshaping customer relationships, offering recurring revenue and loyalty opportunities.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Arcelik, now Beko Corp, has become the world's number two home appliance company after integrating Whirlpool's EMEA markets.
Whirlpool's EMEA markets were acquired by Arcelik, now Beko Corp, to become the world's number two home appliance company.
Haier's recycling plant is mentioned as an example of how circularity initiatives will be key to a company's sustainability push.
